source: Unreal/Khaos BP_Lilly MetaHuman LOD1 body/face plus real Fab long black ponytail mesh and exported MetaHuman top

hair.asset: Game-Ready 3D Female Hair Realistic Ponytail Model

outfit.asset: The Marilla Top for MetaHumans - Parametric Outfit

notes: No 2D proxy, no placeholder mesh, no procedural JS avatar.
The visible hair is the real Fab ponytail OBJ converted into GLB and merged into the browser mesh.
Pose buttons load generated GLB pose variants made from the same deployed real mesh vertices.
Camera panes support direct mouse left-drag orbit rotation, mousewheel zoom, and double-click reset on the real GLB.

Camera 02 is a Sheena Parveen reference video feed; Camera 01 now loads metahuman-sheena-match.glb: the same real MetaHuman body/face/hair GLB recolored to warm brown hair and rust-orange presenter wardrobe, with a real GLB dress-extension mesh added for source-presenter likeness.
Camera 02 now draws a live MediaPipe source-joint overlay from assets/sheena-reference-joints.json so Cam 01 motion can be compared against the presenter reference movement.
Camera 01 now draws an avatar-joint analysis overlay using model-viewer hotspots projected over the real GLB, so the target mesh pose can be compared with the Cam 02 source joints.
In the normal sidebar UI, the Camera 01 joint overlay uses the same CSS transform as the real model-viewer element so projected joints stay aligned with the shifted GLB camera feed.
Camera 01 avatar joints are strict DOM hotspot projections over the real GLB; fallback 2D skeleton points are disabled so misprojected placeholders are never drawn.

The Camera 01 joint canvas is synchronized to the model-viewer transform every draw and is repainted from actual hotspot DOM positions after layout so every rendered frame stays attached to the visible GLB.
Lower-body Camera 01 avatar anchors were recalibrated from model-viewer surface picks on the real GLB so hips, knees, ankles, and feet no longer hang below the avatar mesh.

Camera 01 avatar joints are rebuilt every frame from model-viewer positionAndNormalFromPoint surface hits on the visible real GLB; the overlay no longer uses fixed hotspot coordinates for anatomical placement.
Camera 01 uses real model-viewer orientation/camera motion to mimic the reference timeline without blanking the mesh.
Current browser GLB is marked below cinematic VFX photoreal quality until face, eye, hairline, groom, and material gates pass.
The old Khaos bob hair component is not included in this build.
The shipped GLB is browser-validated through Chromium model-viewer before deploy.
LOD0 was rejected because the Unreal GLB exporter emitted invalid index data for browser viewers; LOD1 is the first browser-valid MetaHuman mesh export.
